name: Glide No-Code App Builder API
what_it_does: No-code app builder with API — enabling teams to create mobile and web applications from Google Sheets, Excel, Airtable, or Glide Tables as data sources, with visual app builder, AI-powered features, workflow automation, and user authentication, all without writing code.
use_cases: Querying Glide app user activity and usage analytics via API
Managing Glide app data via Tables API for dynamic data-driven applications
Triggering Glide workflow actions from external systems via API integration
Reading and writing app records for external data synchronization
Integrating Glide-built apps with enterprise systems via API webhooks
not_for: Complex web applications with advanced UI (use React or low-code like OutSystems)
Enterprise process automation (use Nintex or Appian for BPM)
Marketing websites and content management (use Webflow or Squarespace)
best_when: A business team wants to rapidly build internal mobile apps for field workers, inspections, or operations using existing Google Sheets or Airtable data without engineering resources.
avoid_when: You need complex business logic, customer-facing high-traffic apps, enterprise BPM, or applications with advanced UI/UX beyond what a no-code builder provides.

auth.notes: API key from Glide account settings. Bearer token auth in X-API-Key or Authorization header. App-scoped access. Webhook for user action events. No OAuth2. Google Sheets OAuth for data source. Airtable API key for Airtable data source.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 500 row limit, Glide branding
pricing.notes: San Francisco-based company. Founded 2018. Raised $20M+ Series A. Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund. Google Sheets as data source differentiator — widely familiar to non-technical teams. AI column generation built-in. Used heavily for internal tools, field service apps, inspections, and event management. 50,000+ apps built on Glide globally.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for tables, rows, users, and workflow triggers. API key auth. Good documentation. App configuration is in Glide builder UI — API manages data and triggers actions. Google Sheets OAuth required for Sheets-backed apps. Webhook for user events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Row reads idempotent. Table reads idempotent. Row creation not idempotent — use row ID lookup before creation.
known_agent_gotchas: Google Sheets OAuth required for Sheets-backed apps — two auth systems to manage
App logic and UI built in Glide builder — API only accesses data layer
Free tier row limit (500) insufficient for production operational apps
Row IDs in Glide are internal — manage ID-to-record mapping for external sync
AI columns are Glide-generated — prompt-based, not programmatically configurable

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. API key auth. App user data and operational records can include PII — implement data minimization. Google Sheets OAuth requires careful permission scoping. Field-service app data may include location and employee PII.
